freeLimitedNotice: In the free version, only voltage and current measurements are available.

GuideStep1:
1. Save measurement
Enter values and continue with "Next String". The value will be saved. You can add a note or a photo.
GuideStep2:
2. Scan values (OCR)
Press the scanner icon and take a picture of the meter display. Values will be filled in automatically.
GuideStep3:
3. Take photo of string
Press the camera icon. The photo will be saved with a label (e.g. “2-B1”). Label can be set in settings.
GuideStep4:
4. Export to TXT
Document icon → enter name → export to text.
GuideStep5:
5. Export to table (1 column)
Grid icon → all data in one column.
GuideStep6:
6. Export to table (multiple columns)
Statistics icon → each value in its own column.
GuideStep8:
8. Settings
Here you set critical values, labeling mode, number of cables, languages, what to measure and photo labels.
plusSubscriptionNote: Plus version is an auto-renewing subscription. Price: €3.99 / month.
Numeric mode allows labeling by numbers,: Numeric mode allows labeling by numbers, e.g. 1-500 or 1.1-1.20
GuideStep7:
7. Saved measurements
The folder icon allows you to edit values, notes, and delete entries. Use "Recalculate Errors" to recalculate errors.
exportLangFixedToEnglishFree: Export language is fixed to English in the free version.
ocr.title: OCR Settings
ocr.tip_priority: Choose sorting and meaning based on how your measured values match the position or value on the display.
ocr.free_disabled: Editing OCR settings is not available in the free version. Unlock editing with the Plus version!
ocr.detectWhat_desc: Select which quantities the OCR should detect. You can select multiple options (voltage, current, resistance).
ocr.detectWhat_tip: It is recommended to select all if your meter displays all quantities.
ocr.sortBy_desc: Specifies how OCR results will be assigned to quantities (e.g. by display position, value size, or appearance order).
ocr.sortBy_tip: Most meters display values in fixed order. If not, use value size instead.
ocr.valueMap_desc: Define which quantity corresponds to each position on the display. For example, top = voltage, middle = current, bottom = resistance.
ocr.valueMap_tip: Adjust order according to your meter.
ocr.valuePriority_desc: Define whether the highest number should be considered voltage, the middle as current, and the lowest as resistance.
ocr.valuePriority_tip: Recommended for meters without fixed layout.
ocr.title: OCR Settings
ocr.tips_help2: next to each setting for detailed explanation and recommendations.
ocr.free_disabled: Editing OCR settings is not available in the free version. Unlock editing in the Plus version!
ocr.autoContrast_desc: Automatically increases the image contrast for better readability.
ocr.autoContrast_tip: Recommended if the meter display is blurry or low-contrast.
ocr.autoCrop_desc: Automatically crops the image to include only the relevant area.
ocr.manualCrop_desc: Manually enter the coordinates of the area to recognize.
ocr.backlightDetection_desc: Automatically detects the display’s backlight color.
ocr.backlightDetection_tip: Helps adjust contrast and color filters.
ocr.backlightColor_desc: Manually set the backlight color of the meter display.
ocr.backlightColor_tip: Choose a color that matches your device.
ocr.gammaCorrection_desc: Adjusts brightness using a nonlinear gamma curve.
ocr.gammaCorrection_tip: Recommended in dark environments or with dim backlights.
ocr.manualGamma_desc: Enter a custom gamma value for fine-tuning brightness.
ocr.fallbackEngine_desc: Uses an alternative OCR engine if the main one fails.
ocr.fallbackEngine_tip: Helpful when the main OCR cannot detect text.
ocr.denoise_desc: Reduces image noise to improve text recognition.
ocr.denoise_tip: Useful for blurry or noisy input.
ocr.blacklist_desc: Enter characters that should be ignored during recognition.
ocr.blacklist_tip: Useful when your meter shows unwanted symbols.
ocr.considerContext_desc: OCR will consider surrounding text and layout when interpreting values.
ocr.considerContext_tip: Improves recognition with complex screen backgrounds.
ocr.customOrder_desc: Enter a custom order of quantities, e.g. V-A-Ω.
ocr.customOrder_tip: Defines what quantities OCR should detect and in what order.
ocr.displayType_desc: Select the type of display your meter uses (light on dark, dark on light, auto).
ocr.expectedValues_desc: Specify the range or type of expected values (e.g. 0–1000 V).
ocr.expectedValues_tip: Improves recognition accuracy when limited range is known.
ocr.ignoreDuplicates_desc: If the same value appears more than once, only one will be used.
ocr.ignoreDuplicates_tip: Useful if the meter shows repeated values.
ocr.invertColors_tip: Use when the meter uses an inverted display mode.
ocr.lightMode_desc: Choose the lighting conditions during photo capture.
ocr.lightMode_tip: Adjusts OCR parameters for better results.
ocr.manualContrast_desc: Manually adjust the image contrast level.
ocr.manualContrast_tip: Useful for meters with low display contrast.
ocr.meterType_desc: Select whether the meter is horizontally or vertically oriented.
ocr.minTextLength_desc: Specifies the minimum number of characters to consider as valid value.
ocr.minTextLength_tip: Prevents errors from recognizing partial values.
ocr.ocrAttempts_desc: How many times OCR should retry to improve the result.
ocr.ocrAttempts_tip: Helps in poor image quality scenarios.
ocr.ocrLanguage_desc: Select the language OCR should use during recognition.
ocr.ocrTimeout_desc: Maximum time (in seconds) allowed for OCR to run.
ocr.ocrTimeout_tip: Prevents long waits when OCR fails.
ocr.perspectiveCorrection_tip: Useful when the meter is photographed at an angle.
ocr.regex_desc: Use a regular expression to filter OCR results.
ocr.regex_tip: E.g., restrict to values with decimal point.
ocr.resize_desc: Resize the image before OCR processing.
ocr.resize_tip: Smaller resolution speeds up OCR; larger may improve accuracy.
ocr.resultOrder_desc: Defines how detected values should be ordered.
ocr.resultOrder_tip: E.g., by screen position or numerical value.
ocr.rotation_desc: Rotate the image by a specified angle before OCR.
ocr.valueMapping_desc: Defines which quantity corresponds to which detected value.
ocr.valueMapping_tip: Use when the display layout is unusual.
ocr.whatToDetect_desc: Select which value types should be detected by OCR.
ocr.whitelist_desc: Only allow specified characters during recognition.
ocr.whitelist_tip: Limits recognition to certain types of characters.
ocr.resultOrderMapping: Assign quantities to positions (top, middle, bottom)
ocr.valuePriorityMapping: Assign quantities to values (highest, middle, lowest)
ocr.autoDecimal_desc: Automatically adds a decimal point when there is a larger space between digits.
ocr.autoDecimal_tip: Useful if OCR sometimes omits decimal points in numbers.
ocr.useBinarization_desc: Converts the image to black & white using a threshold value.
ocr.binarizationThreshold_desc: Sets the threshold (0–255) for deciding black or white.
ocr.useAdaptiveThreshold_desc: Threshold is determined locally for different parts of the image.
ocr.decimalSeparator_desc: Choose whether to use a dot or comma as a decimal separator.
ocr.autoEqualizeHistogram_desc: Enhances contrast by adjusting the image histogram.
ocr.debugOverlay_desc: Displays debug graphics for OCR directly on the image.
ocr.autoRotateIfUnclear_desc: Attempts to rotate the image if OCR produces nonsensical results.
ocr.valueFormat_desc: Enter the order of measurements on the display, e.g. "V-A-Ω".
ocr.smartDecimalFallback_desc: Attempts to add a missing decimal point in numbers.
ocr.smartDecimalFallback_tip: Useful if OCR often ignores the dot.
ocr.ocrConfidenceThreshold_desc: Result will be used only if this confidence is reached.
ocr.resampleWidth_desc: Changes image width before recognition (in pixels).
ocr.filterNoise_desc: Attempts to remove noise and artifacts from the image.
ocr.ocrTestMode_desc: Enables detailed logging and output during OCR.
ocr.ocrEngine_desc: Choose the OCR engine: default, backup, experimental.
ocr.detectTypes_desc: Choose which types (voltage, current, resistance) to recognize.
GuideStep1: 1. Saving a measurement. Enter the values and select “Next String”. The measurement is stored and a note or photo can be attached if needed.
GuideStep2: 2. Scanning values (OCR). Use the scanner icon to take a photo of the multimeter display. Recognized values are filled in automatically.
GuideStep3: 3. Taking a photo of the string. Use the camera icon to capture the string. The photo is stored together with a label (for example “2-B1”).
GuideStep4: 4. Export to TXT. Use the document icon, enter a file name and export the measurements as a text file.
GuideStep5: 5. Export to a spreadsheet (single column). Use the grid icon to export all data into a single column.
GuideStep6: 6. Export to a spreadsheet (multiple columns). Use the statistics icon to export each value into its own column.
GuideStep7: 7. Saved measurements. Use the folder icon to open existing measurements for editing values or notes, or for deletion. The “Recalculate Errors” button recalculates all errors.
GuideStep8: 8. Settings. In Settings, limit values, labeling, number of cables, languages, measured quantities and photo labels can be configured.
customRangeHelpMix: You can combine using commas: A1–A3, C1, B2.1–B2.8
customRangeOrderHint: Applies to A1–C4 and A1.1–D1.20. Choose whether the number or the letter should change first.
